prompt: A simple pomodoro timer web app. Start, pause, and reset a 25 minute timer. Track and display the number of completed sessions today, persisted client-side only (localStorage), no backend needed.

A landing page for my home bakery: what we bake, weekly menu, opening hours, a photo gallery section, and a contact section with our phone and address. No ordering, no accounts — just a beautiful static page.
A markdown notes app that runs entirely in my browser: create, edit, delete and search notes, live markdown preview, everything saved in localStorage. No login, no server.

A cooking unit converter web app: convert between cups, grams, ounces, ml and tablespoons for common ingredients like flour, sugar and butter, with a searchable ingredient list. Client-side only, no backend.
A simple to-do list app where I can sign in and only see my own tasks
A CRM to track my sales leads with notes and follow-up dates
A team expense tracker where staff submit expenses and an admin approves them
A booking page for my barbershop where customers pick an available time slot
A URL shortener HTTP API: POST a long URL to get a short code, GET the short code to be redirected, and a JSON endpoint listing my links with click counts.
A command-line tool I can run on my own computer that removes duplicate rows from a CSV file and writes the cleaned file next to the original. No web app, no hosting — just a tool I download and run.
